blocks.text: DTC P0685: ECU power circuit/internal circuit failure (power supply system)

The PGM-FI ECU does not stop all functions immediately after the ignition is turned off (LOCK) mode, but shuts off the power supply by itself after completing the prescribed process according to the requirements of each device or system (self shutdown function). Relay 1 is controlled to shut down the PGM-FI ECU power supply. During normal PGM-FI ECU shutdown, the CPU performs the necessary processing, then turns off the PGM-FI main relay 1 to reduce the power supply voltage to the PGM-FI ECU, thereby completely stopping PGM-FI ECU operation. When the power supply voltage to the PGM-FI ECU drops and the PGM-FI ECU stops operating without following the proper shutdown procedures, The PGM-FI ECU determines that the PGM-FI main relay 1 control circuit has failed and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
If the PGM-FI ECU shuts down without following the proper shutdown procedure.

items.text: Disconnection (including coupler disconnection or looseness) of the cord (FI MAIN RLY CL-line) between PGM-FI ECU and PGM-FI MAIN RELAY 1
Disconnection of the cord (IG1 FUEL PUMP line) between the PGM-FI ECU and fuse box (including disconnected or loose coupler)
Poor connection of PGM-FI ECU ground line

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
